requirements.requirementText: Issuer must provide audited financial statements for at least 2 years prior to IPO
requirements.guidanceText: Financial statements must be audited by a Canadian or US GAAP auditor. Statements must be no older than 120 days at filing.

requirements.requirementText: Issuer must establish an Audit Committee with at least 3 independent directors
requirements.guidanceText: All members of Audit Committee must be independent per Instrument 52-110. At least one member must be a financial expert.

requirements.requirementText: Issuer must establish a Compensation Committee with majority independent directors
requirements.guidanceText: Recommended best practice. Committee should oversee executive compensation and incentive plans.

requirements.requirementText: IPO prospectus must follow Form NI 41-101F1 format as prescribed by Canadian securities regulators
requirements.guidanceText: Form NI 41-101F1 is the standard form for prospectuses in most Canadian provinces. Must include mandatory disclosure about executive compensation, risk factors, use of proceeds, and corporate structure.

requirements.requirementText: If issuer has material exposure to foreign currencies, must disclose currency risks explicitly
requirements.guidanceText: Disclose % revenue exposure by currency, hedging strategies, and potential impact of currency fluctuations.

requirements.requirementText: Legal counsel and auditor must provide formal consents to be named in prospectus
requirements.guidanceText: Consents must be in writing and accompany the prospectus filing. Professional advisors cannot issue 'negative' opinions.

validationRules.errorMessageTemplate: Prospectus must be in PDF format, got {format}
validationRules.remediationGuidance: Convert prospectus to PDF format using standard tools

validationRules.errorMessageTemplate: Prospectus file size ({actual} bytes) exceeds maximum ({max} bytes)
validationRules.remediationGuidance: Compress prospectus or split into multiple documents

validationRules.errorMessageTemplate: Risk Factors section contains only {actual} words, minimum recommended is {min}
validationRules.remediationGuidance: Expand Risk Factors section with specific, material risks relevant to your business
Add all required sections to prospectus per Form NI 41-101F1

items.text: Form NI 41-101F1 Prospectus (PDF, signed by CEO/CFO)
items.guidance: Must be executed and contain all required signatures and dates
Must be prepared in GAAP format and audited by approved firm
Cover at least 2 years of financial history with forward-looking statements
Document establishing Audit Committee with at least 3 independent members
Board approval of IPO, appointment of audit committee, authorization of share issuance
Form 51-102F6 (or equivalent) with executive compensation and governance structure
Written consent from auditors to be named in prospectus
Written consent from legal counsel to be named in prospectus
Letter from lead underwriter committing to IPO underwriting
Opinion from Canadian legal counsel on corporate authority and capitalization
Current certified copies of incorporation documents and bylaws
Complete record of all issued shares, warrants, options, and convertible securities

checklists.description: Comprehensive checklist for TSX IPO filing to ensure all regulatory requirements are met

documentRequirements.description: Audited financial statements for past 2 fiscal years
Legal opinion on corporate status and authority to issue shares

riskFactorRequirements.description: Disclosure of foreign currency exposure and exchange rate risks if applicable
Disclosure of regulatory, environmental, and compliance risks

auditorRequirements.requirementName: Canadian or US GAAP Auditor Required
auditorRequirements.requirementDescription: Financial statements must be audited by firm experienced in Canadian or US GAAP

guidanceTemplates.guidanceText: Risk factors should clearly articulate material risks specific to the issuer's business. Each risk should explain what could go wrong and the potential impact on shareholders. Avoid generic, boilerplate language.
guidanceTemplates.weakExample: We operate in a competitive industry and may face competition from other companies.
guidanceTemplates.weakExampleExplanation: Too vague and generic. Doesn't specify actual competitive threats or financial impact.
guidanceTemplates.strongExample: The market for renewable energy equipment is dominated by three large players (Company A, B, C) who together control 75% of market share. Our lack of established relationships with major utilities and limited capital may prevent us from competing on price or features, potentially limiting our revenue growth to 5-10% annually versus industry average of 20%.
guidanceTemplates.strongExampleExplanation: Specific, quantified, identifies actual competitors and financial impact. Shows deep understanding of market dynamics.
tips: Be specific - identify actual competitors and specific risks
Quantify when possible - use percentages, years, dollars
Focus on risks material to investment decision
Avoid speculation - base on actual business experience
Keep sentences short and clear for readability

guidanceTemplates.guidanceText: Clearly allocate proceeds to specific uses with estimated amounts and timing. Explain how proceeds will be invested and expected benefits. TSX expects conservative estimates.
guidanceTemplates.weakExample: We will use proceeds for general corporate purposes and growth initiatives.
guidanceTemplates.weakExampleExplanation: Too vague. Doesn't specify actual uses or allocation.
guidanceTemplates.strongExample: We will use net proceeds of approximately $50 million as follows: $25 million (50%) for manufacturing facility expansion in Ontario (12-month timeline), $15 million (30%) for working capital and inventory, $7 million (14%) for R&D of next-generation products (ongoing through 2028), and $3 million (6%) for transaction fees and expenses.
guidanceTemplates.strongExampleExplanation: Specific allocation with percentages, timing, and strategic purpose. Shows disciplined capital deployment.
tips: Create detailed allocation table with percentages
Explain strategic rationale for each use
Reference business plan and market opportunity
